jobs.title: Software Engineer AI Agent Design & KNX-Integration
jobs.raw_description: Software Engineer AI Agent Design & KNX-Integration. Anstellung: Vollzeit, Festanstellung. Standort: Wien, 1190. Start: ab sofort. Arbeitsmodell: On-site. Dienstort: Wien 19. Bezirk (Döbling). Enlivion verbindet Gebäudetechnik mit digitaler Innovation, plant und realisiert KNX-Systeme und Elektroanlagen und entwickelt KI-basierte Software, die direkt auf KNX aufsetzt. Als Software Engineer AI-Agent Integration verantwortet die Rolle den gesamten Lebenszyklus von AI-Agenten von Konzeption bis produktiven Betrieb. Aufgaben: Analyse, Modellierung und Design autonomer AI-Agenten für komplexe Geschäftsprozesse; Entwicklung von Bridges, Adapters und Services meist in Python zwischen unterschiedlichen Protokoll-Welten; Containerisieren, Deployen und Monitoren; KNX-Integration und Visualisierung; Definition von Agenten-Architekturen wie ReAct, Plan-and-Execute und Multi-Agent-Systeme; Anbindung externer Dienste via REST, GraphQL und gRPC; Pflege skalierbarer API-Gateways; Implementierung von Agent-to-Agent-Protokollen wie Google A2A, MCP, AutoGen; Design von Multi-Agent-Orchestrierungen inklusive Delegations- und Eskalationslogiken; Integration in International Data Spaces IDS/IDSA und X-Road. Anforderungen: abgeschlossenes Studium in Informatik, Elektrotechnik, Mechatronik oder Praxiserfahrung; 5+ Jahre Softwareentwicklung; solide Python-Kenntnisse inklusive asyncio, Klassen und Decorators; Linux Debian/Ubuntu, SSH, Bash-Scripting; Netzwerk-Grundlagen TCP/UDP, Multicast, Routing, Wireshark/tcpdump; Verständnis für serielle Protokolle RS232, RS485, UART und Modbus TCP/RTU im Real-Einsatz; KNX/EIB praktische Erfahrung mit ETS, Gruppenadressen und DPTs; Verständnis von Agent-to-Agent-Protokollen und Multi-Agent-Koordination; Praxiserfahrung mit REST/GraphQL APIs, API-Design-Patterns und OpenAPI; Erfahrung mit Open-Source-Frameworks für agentenbasierte automation services und jobs; Erfahrung mit Agenten-Frameworks wie LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, Semantic Kernel oder ähnlich; sehr gute Kenntnisse in Python und/oder TypeScript/Node.js; Kenntnisse in Datensouveränität, IDS/IDSA oder ähnlichen Datenraum-Standards; X-Road, React/Vue, LLM-Evaluierung, RAG, Cloud AWS/Azure/GCP und Kubernetes. Gewünschte Bewerbungsunterlagen: Lebenslauf und kurzes Anschreiben.
jobs.salary_info: EUR 3,500 to EUR 5,500 gross monthly depending on experience

jobs.title: Senior Research & Intelligence Analyst AI-Enabled m/f/d
jobs.raw_description: Senior Research & Intelligence Analyst (AI-Enabled) (m/f/d). Company: Körber Pharma GmbH. Location: Vienna, AT. Job ID: 9631. The role supports Strategy and M&A activities by delivering fast-turnaround research on markets, competitors, target companies and industry trends. The person builds, operates and improves reusable AI-enabled research workflows for strategy and M&A projects, market scans and scheduled monitoring runs that produce regularly updated reports. Responsibilities include independent research for Strategy and M&A projects, market analyses, competitive landscapes, target screenings, longlist development, industry and trend analyses; evaluating external signals such as earnings calls, annual reports, capex announcements, market reports, patent data and industry news; producing management-ready research outputs; developing modular AI-enabled workflows such as target screening pipelines, competitor profile generators and earnings call analysis; building scheduled monitoring runs; monitoring output quality including hallucinations, source evaluation and relevance; refining prompts, pipelines and data sources; following the AI tooling and research methods landscape; evaluating new approaches and running hands-on experiments; sharing developments with the team; enabling colleagues through demos, internal sessions and documentation; working with Strategy, M&A and business functions to clarify research questions; and translating complex findings into clear, decision-oriented recommendations for senior stakeholders. Required profile: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Business Informatics, Engineering, Natural Sciences or comparable field; 3+ years of professional experience in research, intelligence, strategy or consulting roles, ideally with exposure to strategic projects and M&A; hands-on experience with modern AI tools in day-to-day work including LLMs, research workflows, prompt engineering and ideally early agentic or API-based setups; demonstrable learning trajectory in AI over the past 6 to 12 months; structured analytical thinking; ability to translate fuzzy research questions into clear methodologies; enjoyment of knowledge sharing; strong understanding of how AI can be applied in strategy and M&A-related environments; excellent English skills; German fluency required; high initiative, curiosity and pragmatic get-it-done mindset. Preferred: Python, APIs, web scraping or workflow automation tools such as n8n, Make or Zapier; AI agent frameworks or RAG-based research systems; scheduled data pipelines or monitoring systems; Life Sciences, MedTech, Pharma or industrial B2B exposure; target screening, due diligence support or competitive intelligence in an M&A context.
jobs.salary_info: Minimum salary EUR 55,000 gross per year based on full-time according to collective agreement; market-rate salary depending on qualifications and professional background
jobs.language_requirements: Excellent English skills; German fluency required

jobs.title: Python Software Engineer - Scientific Computing m/f/d
jobs.raw_description: Python Software Engineer - Scientific Computing (m/f/d). Company: Körber Pharma Austria GmbH. Location: Vienna, AT. Job ID: 9577. For the Vienna office, the company is looking for a Python Software Engineer - Scientific Computing. The team works in biopharmaceutical engineering, combining advanced modeling with modern software engineering to build high-quality scientific tools. Workplace will be Vienna with homeoffice possibility, not fully remote. Responsibilities include designing, implementing and maintaining scalable Python packages that support scientific modeling and engineering workflows; proposing and implementing clean software architectures including class hierarchies, abstraction layers and modular package structures; contributing to a proprietary Python toolchain; collaborating with modeling and engineering teams; and driving best practices in software engineering, code quality, versioning, documentation and packaging. Profile: Master's degree or equivalent experience in computer science, software engineering, computational sciences or related field; strong Python 3 expertise including object-oriented design, abstract base classes, interfaces and scalable package structures; motivation to work collaboratively on a scientific computing library with high coding quality; familiarity with mathematical and statistical modeling concepts is a strong plus, including statistical analysis, predictive modeling, uncertainty quantification, optimization, machine learning and differential equations; ability to work along project deadlines; experience building or contributing to open-source Python libraries is beneficial; solid understanding of testing with pytest, packaging with pip, documentation frameworks and continuous integration; Git and collaborative development workflows; experience with numerical computing libraries such as NumPy, SciPy and pandas. Previous exposure to biotechnology or biopharmaceutical sector is not required. Benefits include flexible working hours, international team, daily meal allowance, annual Wiener Linien/VOR ticket, training opportunities and modern office in Vienna.
jobs.salary_info: Minimum salary according to collective agreement: EUR 40,000 gross per year based on full-time; market-rate salary depending on qualifications and professional background

evaluations.summary: Technically interesting but risky. The AI-agent, Python, LangGraph, REST API, container and RAG parts fit the candidate well. The main problem is that the role is strongly tied to KNX, building automation, networking, serial protocols, Modbus, IDS/IDSA and possibly TypeScript/Node.js. The candidate fits the AI/backend side better than the KNX/building-automation side.
main_match_reasons: Python is central to the role and the candidate has strong Python backend experience.
AI agents, LangGraph, RAG and multi-agent concepts overlap with the candidate's AI/RAG profile.
REST APIs, OpenAPI-style backend integration and service design match the candidate's background.
Linux, Docker/containerization and reliability-focused backend work match parts of the role.
The role values end-to-end lifecycle from concept to productive operation, which fits the candidate's ownership positioning.
The candidate's async/background-processing project experience with RabbitMQ and Redis can support the agent/job orchestration narrative, but should be presented as personal project experience.
main_gaps: No KNX/EIB practical experience is present in the candidate profile.
No real-world Modbus TCP/RTU, RS232, RS485 or UART experience is listed.
Networking requirements such as TCP/UDP, multicast, routing, Wireshark and tcpdump may exceed the candidate's stated profile unless supported by older experience not listed.
IDS/IDSA, X-Road and data sovereignty standards are not in the candidate profile.
TypeScript/Node.js and React/Vue are listed, while the candidate is weaker on frontend and JavaScript/TypeScript.
The role is onsite in Vienna 1190, so it is less flexible than hybrid roles.
required_skills: LangGraph or AutoGen or CrewAI or Semantic Kernel
evaluations.cv_adjustment_notes: Use the AI/backend CV. Emphasize Python, LangGraph, LangChain, RAG, REST APIs, Linux, Docker, backend service ownership, reliability and end-to-end AI system delivery. Include the async RAG project with RabbitMQ and Redis if space allows, clearly as project experience. Do not claim KNX, Modbus, serial protocol, embedded, building automation or professional TypeScript/Node.js experience.
evaluations.interview_prep_notes: Prepare concise explanations for ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, tool routing, multi-agent orchestration, MCP or agent-to-agent protocols, retries, guardrails, LLM evaluation and failure handling. Also prepare an honest answer for the KNX/Modbus/networking gap: no direct KNX production experience, but strong Python integration, APIs, Linux, containerized services and willingness to ramp up fast. Review KNX basics, Modbus TCP vs RTU, RS485, TCP/UDP, multicast and Wireshark/tcpdump basics before applying.
evaluations.risk_notes: This is a mixed-fit role. The AI/backend side is attractive, but the domain requirements are unusually specific. A small company may need someone who can handle both AI software and real building automation protocols from day one. Hiring risk is significant if KNX, Modbus and networking are hard requirements rather than learn-on-the-job areas.
evaluations.next_action: Apply only if the candidate is genuinely interested in smart-building and protocol integration work. If applying, send an AI/backend-focused CV plus a short cover note that is honest about the KNX gap and strong on Python AI-agent systems, APIs, Linux and reliable service design.

evaluations.summary: This is not really a software engineering role. It is a Strategy and M&A research intelligence role using AI tools. The candidate matches the Python, RAG, AI workflow, API and scheduled pipeline preferences, but misses the core job: research, intelligence, strategy, consulting, M&A and management-facing market analysis. Apply only if the candidate wants to pivot away from software engineering into AI-enabled business research.
main_match_reasons: The role values AI-enabled workflows, LLMs, prompt engineering, agentic setups and RAG-based research systems, which overlaps with the candidate's AI/RAG background.
Python, APIs, web scraping and scheduled data pipelines are preferred and match parts of the candidate's technical profile.
The candidate has strong enterprise experience and can communicate with stakeholders.
The candidate's English C2 fits the excellent English requirement.
The candidate's RAG and semantic search experience could help build reusable research workflows and monitor retrieval quality.
main_gaps: The role is centered on Strategy and M&A research, not software engineering.
The candidate profile does not show 3+ years in research, intelligence, strategy or consulting roles.
No direct M&A, target screening, due diligence or competitive intelligence experience is listed.
German fluency is required, while the candidate has professional working German with B2 completed and C1 in progress, which may be below their bar if they mean near-native business German.
The main outputs are management-ready research reports and recommendations, not backend systems, APIs or production software.
Life Sciences, MedTech, Pharma or industrial B2B exposure is only preferred, but not present in the candidate profile.
nice_to_have_skills: Workflow automation tools such as n8n, Make or Zapier
Competitive intelligence in an M&A context
matched_skills: scheduled or background processing from projects
missing_skills: Pharma or life sciences domain exposure
German fluency if interpreted above B2/C1 working level
Business report writing for senior strategy stakeholders
evaluations.cv_adjustment_notes: Do not use the standard software engineering CV unless applying as a stretch. If applying, create a business-facing AI automation version: emphasize AI-enabled research workflows, RAG systems, source quality, hallucination reduction, structured outputs, monitoring, automation and stakeholder communication. Keep Python/backend experience secondary. Do not claim M&A, due diligence, pharma research or strategy consulting experience.
evaluations.interview_prep_notes: Prepare to explain how RAG and AI agents can support market research, competitor monitoring, earnings call analysis, source evaluation and scheduled reporting. Also prepare an honest answer for the lack of M&A and strategy background. Review basic M&A terms, target screening, due diligence, competitive intelligence and pharma market research before any interview.
evaluations.risk_notes: High hiring risk. The candidate is a software engineer, while this role primarily needs a senior research/intelligence analyst with AI tooling skills. The German fluency requirement may also be a blocker if they expect fluent business German for senior stakeholder work. This role could weaken positioning if the candidate's main goal is Python backend or AI engineering.
evaluations.next_action: Skip for a focused software engineering job search. Apply only if the candidate intentionally wants to pivot into AI-enabled strategy research and is willing to compete against consultants, analysts and M&A research profiles.

evaluations.summary: Strong application target. This is a clean Python software engineering role in Vienna with architecture, package design, testing, documentation, Git, pandas and scientific tooling. It is not a perfect AI/RAG match, but it fits the candidate's Python/backend engineering profile better than many niche DACH roles. The main risk is scientific computing depth, especially NumPy, SciPy, mathematical modeling and library/package development expectations.
main_match_reasons: Python is the core technology and the candidate has strong Python backend experience.
The role values clean architecture, modular package structures, code quality, testing, documentation and Git, which fits a reliability-focused software engineer.
The candidate has enterprise engineering experience and can position himself as someone who builds maintainable systems, not just scripts.
The role does not require previous biotechnology or biopharmaceutical sector exposure.
Vienna location with homeoffice possibility is practical for the candidate.
pandas and data-processing experience can support the scientific computing angle, if presented honestly.
main_gaps: The role is scientific computing rather than backend/API-heavy engineering.
No strong professional NumPy, SciPy or scientific Python library development is listed in the candidate profile.
Mathematical and statistical modeling, uncertainty quantification, optimization and differential equations are only listed as pluses, but they may still matter in interviews.
The candidate has a BSc, while the role asks for a Master's degree or equivalent experience.
Open-source scientific Python library contribution is beneficial, but not stated in the candidate profile.
The role does not directly use the candidate's strongest AI/RAG, Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, FastAPI or LangGraph selling points.
required_skills: Master's degree or equivalent experience in a relevant field
Ability to work with scientific modeling and engineering teams
matched_skills: SQL and data workflow experience as supporting background
missing_skills: Strong NumPy experience if not already present
Strong SciPy experience if not already present
evaluations.cv_adjustment_notes: Use a Python backend/software engineering CV, not the AI-heavy version. Emphasize Python architecture, maintainability, testing, data-processing workflows, pandas if truthful, clean APIs, reliability and documentation. Mention AI/RAG only briefly as recent Python engineering experience, not as the main pitch. Do not claim scientific modeling, SciPy or biopharma experience unless it is actually present.
evaluations.interview_prep_notes: Prepare examples around Python package structure, object-oriented design, abstraction layers, testing with pytest, Git workflows, documentation and maintainable code. Review NumPy, SciPy, pandas, packaging with pyproject.toml or setup configuration, pip, virtual environments, type hints and CI basics. Prepare a direct answer for the scientific computing gap: strong Python engineering foundation, data-processing experience and motivation to build robust scientific tooling, while being honest about limited biopharma exposure.
evaluations.risk_notes: The main hiring risk is being seen as a backend/AI engineer rather than a scientific software engineer. The second risk is the Master's or equivalent experience requirement. The candidate should not overstate mathematical modeling or scientific library experience. The profile can still be competitive because the company explicitly values strong Python engineering and says biopharma exposure is not required.
evaluations.next_action: Apply with a Python software engineering CV tailored to clean architecture, package design, testing, documentation and maintainability. Add a short cover note positioning the candidate as a strong Python engineer who can bring production-quality software practices into scientific tooling.

structured_json_raw.summary: Strong application target. This is a clean Python software engineering role in Vienna with architecture, package design, testing, documentation, Git, pandas and scientific tooling. It is not a perfect AI/RAG match, but it fits the candidate's Python/backend engineering profile better than many niche DACH roles. The main risk is scientific computing depth, especially NumPy, SciPy, mathematical modeling and library/package development expectations.
main_match_reasons: Python is the core technology and the candidate has strong Python backend experience.
The role values clean architecture, modular package structures, code quality, testing, documentation and Git, which fits a reliability-focused software engineer.
The candidate has enterprise engineering experience and can position himself as someone who builds maintainable systems, not just scripts.
The role does not require previous biotechnology or biopharmaceutical sector exposure.
Vienna location with homeoffice possibility is practical for the candidate.
pandas and data-processing experience can support the scientific computing angle, if presented honestly.
main_gaps: The role is scientific computing rather than backend/API-heavy engineering.
No strong professional NumPy, SciPy or scientific Python library development is listed in the candidate profile.
Mathematical and statistical modeling, uncertainty quantification, optimization and differential equations are only listed as pluses, but they may still matter in interviews.
The candidate has a BSc, while the role asks for a Master's degree or equivalent experience.
Open-source scientific Python library contribution is beneficial, but not stated in the candidate profile.
The role does not directly use the candidate's strongest AI/RAG, Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, FastAPI or LangGraph selling points.
required_skills: Master's degree or equivalent experience in a relevant field
Ability to work with scientific modeling and engineering teams
matched_skills: SQL and data workflow experience as supporting background
missing_skills: Strong NumPy experience if not already present
Strong SciPy experience if not already present
